description: Symptom decoder — maps common driver-described symptoms to likely causes in plain English. Helps the app translate 'that weird sound' into actionable next steps.

symptoms.plainEnglish: A high-pitched squeal or screech when you press the brake pedal

likelyCauses.cause: Worn brake pads — wear indicator tab is scraping the rotor
likelyCauses.urgency: Schedule service soon — you still have some pad life but not much

likelyCauses.cause: Glazed brake pads — hard braking has hardened the pad surface
likelyCauses.urgency: Can continue driving — might improve with normal braking

likelyCauses.cause: Rust on brake rotors — surface rust from sitting overnight or rain
likelyCauses.urgency: No action needed — will disappear after a few stops

symptoms.plainEnglish: A deep metal-on-metal grinding or growling sound when you brake

likelyCauses.cause: Brake pads completely worn down — metal backing plate is grinding against the rotor
likelyCauses.urgency: Stop driving immediately. You have no friction material left and are damaging the rotors (and possibly calipers)

symptoms.plainEnglish: A high-pitched squeal that happens as you turn the steering wheel

likelyCauses.cause: Low power steering fluid — pump is starving for fluid
likelyCauses.urgency: Check fluid level. A leak might be present

likelyCauses.cause: Worn serpentine belt — belt slipping on power steering pulley
likelyCauses.urgency: Replace belt soon — if it snaps, you lose power steering and alternator

likelyCauses.cause: Power steering pump failing
likelyCauses.urgency: Get diagnosed soon — pump failure can leave you without power steering

symptoms.plainEnglish: A dull thud or clunk sound from the front of the car when you turn

likelyCauses.cause: Worn CV joint — the rubber boot likely tore, letting dirt in and grease out
likelyCauses.urgency: Replace soon — if it fails completely, the axle can separate and your car won't move

likelyCauses.cause: Worn ball joint or tie rod end — suspension component with play
likelyCauses.urgency: Get inspected immediately — failure can cause loss of steering control

likelyCauses.cause: Loose sway bar link — stabilizer bar link has worn out
likelyCauses.urgency: Annoying but not immediately dangerous — replace when convenient

symptoms.plainEnglish: The yellow engine-shaped warning light on your dashboard is lit up

likelyCauses.cause: Loose or faulty gas cap — the most common cause
likelyCauses.urgency: Tighten the gas cap. If light clears after 2-3 drives, no further action needed

likelyCauses.cause: Oxygen sensor failure — sensor reading exhaust gases has failed
likelyCauses.urgency: Replace soon — affects fuel economy, can damage catalytic converter if ignored

likelyCauses.cause: Catalytic converter failure — emissions device is clogged or failing
likelyCauses.urgency: Get diagnosed — car may fail emissions test and lose power

likelyCauses.cause: Mass airflow sensor dirty — sensor measuring incoming air needs cleaning
likelyCauses.urgency: Can try cleaning with MAF cleaner first — cheap fix

likelyCauses.cause: Ignition coil or spark plug failure — cylinder misfire
likelyCauses.urgency: Replace soon — driving with a misfire can damage the catalytic converter

symptoms.plainEnglish: The yellow engine light is blinking rapidly, not staying steady

likelyCauses.cause: Catalyst-damaging misfire — a cylinder is not firing at all, sending raw fuel into the exhaust
likelyCauses.urgency: Stop driving immediately. A flashing CEL means you're destroying the catalytic converter. Tow to a mechanic

symptoms.symptom: Car shakes or vibrates at highway speeds
symptoms.plainEnglish: The steering wheel or seat vibrates noticeably when driving 60+ mph

likelyCauses.cause: Wheels out of balance — tire weights fell off
likelyCauses.urgency: Get tires balanced — inexpensive fix, prevents uneven tire wear

likelyCauses.cause: Bent wheel — hit a pothole and bent the rim
likelyCauses.urgency: Get inspected — may need replacement or repair

likelyCauses.cause: Tire separation or bubble — internal tire damage
likelyCauses.urgency: Replace immediately — tire could blow out at speed

symptoms.symptom: Car pulls to one side while driving
symptoms.plainEnglish: You have to hold the steering wheel at an angle to go straight

likelyCauses.cause: Wheel alignment out of spec
likelyCauses.urgency: Schedule alignment — causes uneven tire wear

likelyCauses.cause: Uneven tire pressure — one tire is low
likelyCauses.urgency: Check and inflate all tires to spec

likelyCauses.cause: Stuck brake caliper — brake not fully releasing on one wheel
likelyCauses.urgency: Get inspected soon — can overheat and damage the wheel bearing

symptoms.symptom: Thumping or rhythmic noise that gets faster with speed
symptoms.plainEnglish: A repetitive 'womp womp womp' sound that speeds up as you accelerate

likelyCauses.cause: Flat spot on tire or tire cupping — worn suspension causing uneven tire wear
likelyCauses.urgency: Get tires inspected — may need replacement and suspension check

likelyCauses.cause: Nail or object stuck in tire
likelyCauses.urgency: Get tire patched — temporary fix possible with plug kit

symptoms.symptom: Whining noise from engine that changes with RPM
symptoms.plainEnglish: A high-pitched whine that goes up and down as you press the gas

likelyCauses.cause: Alternator bearing failing — alternator is grinding internally
likelyCauses.urgency: Get diagnosed — if alternator fails, battery dies and car won't start

likelyCauses.cause: Low power steering fluid — pump whining because it's starved
likelyCauses.urgency: Check fluid level and top off — look for leaks

likelyCauses.cause: Serpentine belt tensioner failing — belt is slipping
likelyCauses.urgency: Replace belt and tensioner soon

symptoms.plainEnglish: You turn the key or push the button and hear rapid clicking, but the engine doesn't turn over

likelyCauses.cause: Dead battery — not enough power to turn the starter
likelyCauses.urgency: Jump start the car. If it starts, drive to a parts store for a free battery test

likelyCauses.cause: Corroded or loose battery terminals
likelyCauses.urgency: Clean terminals with a wire brush and tighten connections

likelyCauses.cause: Faulty starter motor — starter is not engaging the flywheel
likelyCauses.urgency: Needs replacement — tap starter with a hammer as a temporary trick

symptoms.symptom: Car won't start — no sound at all
symptoms.plainEnglish: You turn the key and nothing happens — no clicking, no cranking, just silence

likelyCauses.cause: Completely dead battery — no power at all
likelyCauses.urgency: Jump start or replace battery. Check if lights work to confirm

likelyCauses.cause: Faulty ignition switch or starter relay
likelyCauses.urgency: Needs diagnostic — may need towing

likelyCauses.cause: Security system immobilizer — chip in key not recognized
likelyCauses.urgency: Try spare key. If that works, original key's chip may be damaged

symptoms.symptom: Car overheats — temperature gauge near red
symptoms.plainEnglish: The temperature gauge on the dashboard is reading high, or you see steam from under the hood

likelyCauses.cause: Low coolant level — leak in the cooling system
likelyCauses.urgency: Stop driving immediately. Turn on heater full blast to help cool engine. Check coolant reservoir when cool

likelyCauses.cause: Thermostat stuck closed — coolant not circulating through radiator
likelyCauses.urgency: Needs replacement — relatively cheap fix

likelyCauses.cause: Cooling fan not working — fan relay or motor failed
likelyCauses.urgency: Will overheat at idle/stop-and-go. Get fixed soon

likelyCauses.cause: Water pump failure — coolant not circulating
likelyCauses.urgency: Needs immediate replacement — can cause engine damage

symptoms.plainEnglish: An acrid or sweet smell coming through the air vents when you use the heater or AC

likelyCauses.cause: Oil or coolant leak dripping onto hot exhaust manifold
likelyCauses.urgency: Get inspected — could be a gasket leak. Sweet smell = coolant (head gasket?), burning oil smell = valve cover gasket

likelyCauses.cause: Heater core leaking — coolant leaking inside the cabin
likelyCauses.urgency: Sweet smell + foggy windows = leaking heater core. Needs replacement

likelyCauses.cause: Clutch burning (manual) — riding the clutch while driving
likelyCauses.urgency: Adjust driving habits — if smell persists, clutch may need replacement

symptoms.plainEnglish: The steering wheel or brake pedal pulsates/pulses when you apply the brakes

likelyCauses.cause: Warped brake rotors — rotors are no longer perfectly flat from heat
likelyCauses.urgency: Rotors need resurfacing or replacement. Can be driven but will worsen

symptoms.plainEnglish: Engine revs but the car doesn't accelerate as expected — feels like the transmission is 'catching up'

likelyCauses.cause: Low transmission fluid — leak or hasn't been changed
likelyCauses.urgency: Check fluid level immediately. Continuing to drive can destroy the transmission

likelyCauses.cause: Worn transmission bands or clutches — internal wear
likelyCauses.urgency: Needs transmission service or rebuild — expensive repair

likelyCauses.cause: Faulty transmission control module (TCM) — electronic issue
likelyCauses.urgency: May need reprogramming or replacement — cheaper than a mechanical rebuild

symptoms.plainEnglish: A metallic rattling sound coming from under the hood, especially on acceleration

likelyCauses.cause: Engine knocking / pinging — using fuel with too low octane rating
likelyCauses.urgency: Try higher octane fuel. If it persists, could be carbon buildup or ignition timing issue

likelyCauses.cause: Loose heat shield — metal shield over exhaust manifold is loose
likelyCauses.urgency: Annoying but not dangerous — can be re-secured with a hose clamp or wrench

likelyCauses.cause: Timing chain tensioner failing — chain slapping against guides
likelyCauses.urgency: Get diagnosed immediately — if timing chain jumps, engine can be destroyed

symptoms.symptom: Humming noise that changes with speed
symptoms.plainEnglish: A low droning or humming sound that gets louder or changes pitch as you accelerate

likelyCauses.cause: Worn wheel bearing — bearing inside the hub is failing
likelyCauses.urgency: Replace soon — if it fails, wheel can separate. The noise will get louder when turning one direction

likelyCauses.cause: Cupped / unevenly worn tires — suspension issue causing tire wear pattern
likelyCauses.urgency: May need new tires and alignment check

symptoms.plainEnglish: White or light gray smoke coming from the tailpipe, especially when warm

likelyCauses.cause: Normal condensation — water vapor from cold start (disappears after warming up)
likelyCauses.urgency: No action needed — completely normal in cold weather

likelyCauses.cause: Coolant burning — head gasket failure allowing coolant into combustion chamber
likelyCauses.urgency: Sweet-smelling white smoke = head gasket failure. Stop driving to avoid engine damage

likelyCauses.cause: Cracked engine block or cylinder head
likelyCauses.urgency: Engine likely needs replacement — severe issue

symptoms.symptom: Blue or gray smoke from exhaust
symptoms.plainEnglish: Bluish or gray smoke coming from the tailpipe, especially on acceleration or startup

likelyCauses.cause: Oil burning — worn piston rings or valve seals letting oil into combustion chamber
likelyCauses.urgency: Check oil level frequently. Will need engine rebuild or replacement eventually

likelyCauses.cause: Turbocharger seals failing — oil leaking past turbo seals into exhaust
likelyCauses.urgency: Turbo needs rebuild or replacement — can cause runaway engine if severe

symptoms.plainEnglish: A strong gasoline smell, either inside the cabin or outside the car

likelyCauses.cause: Loose gas cap — most common cause
likelyCauses.urgency: Tighten gas cap until it clicks

likelyCauses.cause: Fuel injector leak — fuel leaking from injector seal
likelyCauses.urgency: Get inspected immediately — fire risk

likelyCauses.cause: EVAP system leak — fuel vapor recovery system has a leak
likelyCauses.urgency: Can trigger check engine light. Get diagnosed
